chapters.title: — Headline: Effect Size and What It Means
— Round 1: Rondagh — Monochorionic Twins and Anastomosis Burden
— Round 2: Champion — AC Extremes in Pregestational Diabetes
— Round 3: Mei-Dan — Spontaneous PTB as Cardiovascular Risk Marker

key_claims.claim: History of stillbirth independently elevates the risk of recurrent stillbirth in subsequent pregnancies after adjusting for known confounders
In monochorionic twins with single fetal demise, the number and diameter of arterio-arterial and veno-venous anastomoses correlate with severe brain injury in the surviving twin
Both small and large fetal abdominal circumference percentiles associate with composite perinatal morbidity in pregnancies complicated by pregestational diabetes
Spontaneous preterm birth is an independent risk factor for future maternal cardiovascular morbidity and should be incorporated into long-term risk counseling
Pre-cerclage amniocentesis may help distinguish mechanical from inflammatory cervical insufficiency and optimize cerclage candidacy in asymptomatic short cervix
